rationale: The citing paper’s core contribution is modeling context effects including the compromise effect, and the cited paper’s main contribution is experimental evidence on when compromise choices are reduced (via private self-awareness and preference fluency). This is a direct construct-level overlap with the citing paper’s stated positioning against prior single-effect context-effect work, so the citation could be theoretically substantive rather than incidental. Given only DOI-level match evidence and no quote context, it should be kept for full relation judgment instead of dropped.
